https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Cool-off period order rejection
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Cool-off period order rejection
Last update: June 23, 2026
OKX is extending the contract cool-off period — currently enforced only in the web/app UI — to all order channels including REST API and WebSocket. Once a user's cool-off period is active, non reduce-only orders on SWAP and FUTURES instruments covered by the cool-off will be rejected server-side. Reduce-only orders remain allowed.
Expected production go-live: July 7, 2026 .
New error code
Code HTTP Status Message 54094 200 Order rejected. The cool-off period is active for the current instId.

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: ELP consolidated order book
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
ELP consolidated order book
Last update: June 23, 2026
To simplify ELP market data integration, OKX is introducing books-elp-all — a consolidated order book that merges non-ELP and currently tradeable ELP liquidity into a single stream, so you no longer need to subscribe to books and books-elp separately and merge them client-side. It is available over both WebSocket and REST, and is expected to be available in the demo environment in mid-July 2026 , with production go-live in late July 2026 .
Added a new WebSocket channel books-elp-all on the /ws/v5/business endpoint ( wss://ws.okx.com:8443/ws/v5/business ). 400 depth levels; initial full snapshot + incremental data every 100 ms. Pushes consolidated order book depth combining non-ELP and currently tradeable ELP liquidity. Non-tradeable ELP orders are filtered out at the platform side. WS / books-elp-all channel
Each element in asks and bids is a 4-element array: [price, totalQty, nonElpQty, orderCount]
price : depth price totalQty : total visible quantity at this price level (non-ELP qty + currently tradeable ELP qty). ELP-enabled takers should use this as the full visible depth. nonElpQty : non-ELP (organic) quantity only. Non-ELP takers should use this field as their available depth. orderCount : number of visible orders at this price level (includes both non-ELP and currently tradeable ELP orders).
Push Data Parameters
Parameter Type Description asks Array of Arrays Order book on sell side. Each element is [price, totalQty, nonElpQty, orderCount] bids Array of Arrays Order book on buy side. Each element is [price, totalQty, nonElpQty, orderCount] ts String Order book generation time, Unix timestamp format in milliseconds, e.g. 1779782400000 prevSeqId Integer Sequence ID of the last sent message. Snapshot uses prevSeqId = -1 . seqId Integer Sequence ID of the current message
Added a new REST endpoint to retrieve consolidated ELP order book: GET / Order book
GET / Consolidated ELP order book
Retrieve the consolidated ELP order book of the instrument, combining non-ELP and currently tradeable ELP liquidity. The data will be updated once every 200 milliseconds.
This endpoint does not return data immediately. Instead, it returns the latest data once the server-side cache has been updated.
Rate limit: 20 requests per 2 seconds
Rate limit rule: IP
Permission: Public
HTTP Request
GET /api/v5/market/books-elp-all
Request Example
GET /api/v5/market/books-elp-all?instId = BTC-USDT-SWAP
Request Parameters
Parameter Type Required Description instId String Yes Instrument ID, e.g. BTC-USDT-SWAP . Only instruments with ELP enabled are supported. sz String No Order book depth per side. Maximum 400 , default 1
Response Example
{ "code" : "0" , "msg" : "" , "data" : [ { "asks" : [ [ "67855.2" , "0.5" , "0.5" , "1" ], [ "67856.0" , "1.3" , "1.0" , "4" ], [ "67860.5" , "0.3" , "0" , "1" ] ], "bids" : [ [ "67854.8" , "1.7" , "1.2" , "3" ], [ "67853.0" , "0.8" , "0.8" , "1" ] ], "ts" : "1779782400000" , "seqId" : 123456 } ] }
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description asks Array of Arrays Order book on sell side. Each element is [price, totalQty, nonElpQty, orderCount] bids Array of Arrays Order book on buy side. Each element is [price, totalQty, nonElpQty, orderCount] ts String Order book generation time, Unix timestamp format in milliseconds, e.g. 1779782400000 seqId Integer Sequence ID of the current message

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: ELP taker access expanded to all order types
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
ELP taker access expanded to all order types
Last update: June 23, 2026
The isElpTakerAccess order parameter is being expanded to support all order types (previously ioc only) and is now also accepted on the amend-order endpoints. This change is expected to be available in the demo environment in mid-July 2026 , with production go-live in late July 2026 .
Updated the description of request parameter isElpTakerAccess to reflect expanded order type support and amend behavior: POST / Place order POST / Place multiple orders POST / Amend order POST / Amend multiple orders WS / Place order WS / Place multiple orders WS / Amend order WS / Amend multiple orders
Request Parameters
Parameter Type Required Description isElpTakerAccess Boolean No The default value is false . When set to true , the order can access ELP liquidity. Applicable to all order types. A speedbump mechanism applies to all order types except post_only when isElpTakerAccess is true ; post_only orders are exempt from the speedbump when placing order. isElpTakerAccess is also accepted on the amend-order endpoints and does NOT inherit from the original order — it must be explicitly re-specified on each amend request (omitting it on an amend means false for that amend). On amend, the speedbump applies to all order types including post_only ; to amend a post_only order without the speedbump, omit isElpTakerAccess on that amend.

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: OKUSD subscribe, redeem, and limits — New API endpoints
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
OKUSD subscribe, redeem, and limits — New API endpoints
Last update: June 23, 2026
OKX is adding V5 API support for OKUSD. API users can now programmatically subscribe USDT to receive OKUSD at a 1:1 rate, redeem OKUSD back to USDT via fast (real-time) or standard (D+5) redemption, and query remaining daily limits. This change is expected to go live on July 2, 2026 .
Get OKUSD limits
Added GET /api/v5/finance/okusd/limits
Requires API Key with read permission. Rate limit: 2 requests per 2 seconds per UID.
Response parameters
Parameter Type Description subLimit Object Subscription limit information > maxSubAmt String Maximum subscribable amount for today (USDT). Equal to min(personalDailyLimit - personalUsedAmt, platformDailyLimit - platformUsedAmt) > personalDailyLimit String User's daily subscription limit based on VIP tier (USDT) > personalUsedAmt String User's subscription amount used today (USDT) > platformDailyLimit String Platform daily subscription limit (USDT) > platformUsedAmt String Platform subscription amount used today (USDT) fastRedeemLimit Object Fast Redemption limit information > personalDailyLimit String User's daily fast redemption limit based on VIP tier (OKUSD) > personalUsedAmt String User's fast redemption amount used today (OKUSD) > platformDailyLimit String Platform daily fast redemption limit (OKUSD) > platformUsedAmt String Platform fast redemption amount used today (OKUSD) > feeRate String Fast redemption fee rate as a decimal ratio (e.g. "0.001" = 0.1%) stdRedeemLimit Object Standard Redemption (D+5) limit information > personalDailyLimit String User's daily standard redemption limit based on VIP tier (OKUSD) > personalUsedAmt String User's standard redemption amount used today (OKUSD) > platformDailyLimit String Platform daily standard redemption limit (OKUSD) > platformUsedAmt String Platform standard redemption amount used today (OKUSD) > feeRate String Standard redemption fee rate as a decimal ratio (e.g. "0.00025" = 0.025%) ts String Server timestamp (Unix ms)
Subscribe OKUSD
Added POST /api/v5/finance/okusd/subscribe
Requires API Key with trade permission. Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds per UID.
Request parameters
Parameter Type Required Description amt String Yes Amount of USDT to subscribe. Minimum: 1. Up to 8 decimal places.
Response parameters
Parameter Type Description ordId String Order ID ccy String Subscription currency, always "USDT" amt String Actual USDT amount subscribed okusdAmt String OKUSD amount credited (equals amt ; 1:1 rate, no subscription fee) state String Order state: success / pending / failed ts String Order creation time (Unix ms)
Redeem OKUSD
Added POST /api/v5/finance/okusd/redeem
Requires API Key with trade permission. Rate limit: 1 request per 2 seconds per UID.
Fast Redemption settles in real-time. Standard Redemption settles within D+5 business days.
Request parameters
Parameter Type Required Description amt String Yes Amount of OKUSD to redeem. Minimum: 1. Up to 8 decimal places. redeemType String Yes Redemption type. 1 = Fast Redemption (real-time); 2 = Standard Redemption (D+5)
Response parameters
Parameter Type Description ordId String Order ID ccy String Redemption currency, always "OKUSD" amt String OKUSD amount redeemed fee String Fee charged (USDT), truncated to 8 decimal places usdtAmt String USDT amount received = amt - fee , truncated to 8 decimal places redeemType String Redemption type: 1 = Fast Redemption; 2 = Standard Redemption state String Order state: processing / success / failed estSettlementTime String Estimated settlement time (Unix ms). Fast: current time; Standard: submission time + 5 business days ts String Order creation time (Unix ms)
Added new error codes
Error Code HTTP Status Error Message 51763 200 Your account does not meet the VIP tier requirement for this product 51764 200 Insufficient balance 51765 200 Exceed your remaining daily quota of {x} USDT 51766 200 Platform daily subscription limit reached 51767 200 System maintenance, please retry 51768 200 Exceed your remaining fast redemption quota of {x} OKUSD 51769 200 Platform fast redemption limit reached 51770 200 Exceed your remaining standard redemption quota of {x} OKUSD 51771 200 Platform standard redemption limit reached 51772 200 Instant redemption pool insufficient 51773 200 Feature not available in your region 51774 200 OKUSD API is under maintenance

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Price limit XYZ parameters for instruments
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Price limit XYZ parameters for instruments
Last update: June 23, 2026
To enable API users to dynamically retrieve contract price-limit parameters instead of hardcoding them, OKX is exposing the price-limit X, Y, and Z parameters via the instruments endpoints. The computed price limits derived from these parameters are available via GET / Get limit price . Refer to Price limit rules for more details. This change is expected to be available in the demo environment on June 24, 2026 , with production go-live on June 30, 2026 .
Added new response parameters initPxLmtPct , floatPxLmtPct , maxPxLmtPct to: GET / Get instruments (public) GET / Get instruments (private)
Response Parameters
Parameter Type Description initPxLmtPct String Initial price-limit band applied during the first 10 minutes after contract listing. Decimal percentage, e.g. 0.05 = 5%. Applicable to SPOT / MARGIN / SWAP / FUTURES ; returns "" for OPTION and EVENTS . floatPxLmtPct String Floating price-limit band during normal trading. Decimal percentage, e.g. 0.03 = 3%. Applicable to SPOT / MARGIN / SWAP / FUTURES ; returns "" for OPTION and EVENTS . maxPxLmtPct String Maximum price-limit cap (hard ceiling). Decimal percentage, e.g. 0.15 = 15%. Applicable to SPOT / MARGIN / SWAP / FUTURES ; returns "" for OPTION and EVENTS .

https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.title: Signal Clone — New API endpoint
https://www.okx.com/docs-v5/log_en#upcom.content:
Signal Clone — New API endpoint
Last update: May 14, 2026
OKX is adding API support for the Signal Clone (Order Sharing) feature. API traders can now generate a universal sharing link programmatically by passing an order ID. The link encodes the order parameters (instrument, side, leverage, price, TP/SL, etc.) server-side. Recipients who open the link will have those parameters prefilled in the OKX App trading panel.
This feature is scoped to USDT-margined perpetual contracts. Signal Clone must be enabled for the account.
Create signal link
Create signal link
A new endpoint POST /api/v5/copytrade/create-sgl-link is added. Pass orderId and instId in the request body. The order must belong to the requesting account. Only USDT-margined perpetual contracts ( instId ending in -USDT-SWAP ) are supported. Other instrument types will return an error. Returns a shortLink — a universal OKX App link that can be shared with others to prefill the trading panel with the order parameters. Rate limit: 10 requests per second per user.
Request example
POST /api/v5/copytrade/create-sgl-link
body { "orderId" : "3556007031710728192" , "instId" : "ADA-USDT-SWAP" }
Request parameters
Parameter Type Required Description orderId String Yes Order ID. Must belong to the requesting account. instId String Yes Instrument ID, e.g. BTC-USDT-SWAP . Only USDT-margined perpetuals are supported.
Response example
{ "code" : "0" , "data" : [ { "shortLink" : "https://www.okx.com/ul/1xJ7nV" } ], "msg" : "" }
Response parameters
Parameter Type Description shortLink String Universal sharing link. Recipients can open this link in the OKX App to have the order parameters prefilled in the trading panel.
